name: PipeEvent - Pipe Connected
definition: Sysmon Event ID 18 records a named pipe connection between a client and a server.
trigger_scenarios: Sysmon emits this event when a named pipe connection is made. It complements Event ID 17 by showing connection activity rather than only pipe creation.

key_fields.explanation: The connected named pipe. MITRE's IPC detection strategy uses anomalous named-pipe patterns, including \\.\\pipe\\svcctl, as an environment-specific detection element.
Identifies the process connecting to the pipe and provides a stable join key to Sysmon Event ID 1 for command-line and parent-process context.
Shows the Sysmon rule that matched, if rule names are configured, and helps explain why this pipe connection was captured.

false_positives: Approved administration, management agents, and deployment tools can generate this telemetry.
Baseline expected hosts, signed binaries, and change windows before suppression.
detection_notes: Alert when a process from C:\\Users\\, C:\\ProgramData\\, or C:\\Windows\\Temp connects to a rare named pipe or an administrative IPC pattern such as \\.\\pipe\\svcctl outside an approved administration workflow. Microsoft documents Event 18 as a named pipe connection between a client and server; MITRE DET0493 maps anomalous named-pipe creation or access to T1559 Inter-Process Communication. Correlate ProcessGuid to Event 1 and compare the Image, parent process, and host role with the named-pipe baseline.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 18
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=18 | table _time, host, Image, ProcessGuid
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 01:00:00.000
PipeName: \\.\\pipe\\svcctl
Image: C:\\Windows\\Temp\\client.exe
ProcessGuid: {REDACTED}
RuleName: suspicious_pipe

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 18
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 18
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the documented fields.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 18
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=18
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Add the documented event-specific conditions after field validation.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 18
assumptions: Confirm the Sysmon provider because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw fields because parsed names vary.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 18
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 18
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 18
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.

value_references.meaning: A process connected to a named pipe.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the full event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The connected named-pipe path used for client/server correlation.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret PipeName with the full event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 18 mean?
faqs.answer: A client process connected to a named pipe server.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 18 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Named-pipe connections are normal inter-process communication.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 18 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Correlate PipeName, Image and ProcessGuid with the pipe creator and expected application behavior.
